name: Route of the 3 Waterfalls
name_original: La Ruta de las 3 Cascadas — Circular por la Albufera de Anna
description: Hike the easy 3.5-mile Route of the 3 Waterfalls circular trail near Anna, exploring stunning cascades, natural pools, and the tranquil Albu
region_intro: Hiking around Anna features a landscape characterized by its natural water features, including multiple waterfalls and the tranquil Albufera de Anna. The region offers a mix of easy paths winding through lush areas and more moderate trails with varied terrain. Hikers can explore gorges, natural pools, and scenic routes that highlight the area's hydrological beauty. Best hiking trails around Anna The most popular hiking route is The Route of the 3 Waterfalls — Circular by the Albufera de Anna, a 3.5 miles (5.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 32 minutes to complete. This easy circular path showcases the region's prominent waterfalls and the serene Albufera. Another top favourite among local hikers is Gorgo de la Escalera – Salto Waterfall loop from Anna, an easy 3.6 miles (5.8 km) path. This route leads through a scenic gorge to the Salto Waterfall, offering refreshing natural pools and varied terrain. Local hikers also love the Salto Waterfall – Vikings' Waterfall loop from Anna, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail leading through a landscape rich with waterfalls, often completed in about 3 hours 22 minutes. Hiking around Anna is defined by its waterfalls, natural pools like Gorgo de la Escalera, and the tranquil Albufera, with routes ranging from easy walks to moderate excursions. The network offers options for different ability levels, with 16 easy and 11 moderate trails. The routes in Anna are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from almost 200 reviews. More than 800 hikers have used komoot to explore Anna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails can I find around Anna?
A: Anna offers a diverse network of over 25 hiking trails. These routes range from easy strolls to more moderate excursions, ensuring options for various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Anna?
A: The terrain around Anna is characterized by its natural water features. You'll find paths winding through lush areas, alongside rivers, and leading to gorges and waterfalls. Some trails feature varied terrain, including natural pools and scenic routes that highlight the area's hydrological beauty.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Anna?
A: Yes, Anna features several popular circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e818004947/the-route-of-the-3-waterfalls-circular-by-the-albufera-de-anna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the 3 Waterfalls — Circular by the Albufera de Anna&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.5-mile loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10350409" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorgo de la Escalera – Salto Waterfall loop from Anna&lt;/a&gt; is another easy circular path.

Q: What are the main natural attractions to see while hiking in Anna?
A: Anna is renowned for its stunning water-related attractions. Hikers can explore multiple waterfalls like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4586632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vikings' Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4586622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaspar Waterfall and Pool&lt;/a&gt;, as well as the impressive gorge of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorgo de la Escalera&lt;/a&gt;. The tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4070836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera de Anna&lt;/a&gt; is also a prominent feature.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Anna suitable for families?
A: Many trails in Anna are suitable for families, especially the easier routes that showcase the region's natural beauty without significant difficulty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e818004947/the-route-of-the-3-waterfalls-circular-by-the-albufera-de-anna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the 3 Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent easy option for all ages.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Anna?
A: The hiking routes in Anna are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from almost 200 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the beautiful waterfalls, natural pools, and the serene atmosphere of the Albufera.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Anna?
A: Given Anna's focus on water features, spring and autumn are often ideal for hiking, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can be warm, but the natural pools and waterfalls provide refreshing spots to cool down.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes available?
A: While many trails are easy to moderate, some routes offer a longer experience. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10350596" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto Waterfall – Vikings' Waterfall loop from Anna&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.9-mile trail that takes around 3 hours 22 minutes to complete, providing a more extended exploration of the area's waterfalls.

Q: Where can I find natural pools for swimming after a hike?
A: The region is famous for its natural pools. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorgo de la Escalera&lt;/a&gt; is a well-known spot with refreshing natural pools, often accessible via trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10350409" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorgo de la Escalera – Salto Waterfall loop from Anna&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots on the trails?
A: Yes, the trails often lead to scenic spots, particularly those overlooking the waterfalls and gorges. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5825175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Salto de Chella Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is a notable location offering panoramic views of the surrounding landscape.

Q: How can I access the trailheads, particularly regarding parking?
A: Specific parking information varies by trailhead. It is generally advisable to check local signage or online resources for designated parking areas near popular starting points like those for the Gorgo de la Escalera or the Albufera de Anna.
tips:
The lake is a protected area of flora and fauna. Even so, it has parking lots and a recreational area.

It is an ideal place to spend a quiet day with the family.

Gorgo Gaspar in Anna is a small waterfall and natural area located next to the town.

It is known for being the first stop on the famous "Three Waterfalls Route" of Anna. It was formerly used to power a textile factory.

The Vikings Waterfall, located in Anna (Valencia), is one of the three natural gems that form part of the famous Anna Waterfalls Route, along with the Gorgo Gaspar and the Gorgo de la Escalera.
It is a vertical waterfall of great beauty, surrounded by vegetation and rocks, ideal for nature and photography lovers.

The Salto de Anna is a spectacular waterfall of about 20 meters in the municipality of Anna (Valencia), where the water cascades down with great force. It forms part of the Three Waterfalls Route and stands out for its natural beauty and lush surroundings.

The Gorgo de la Escalera, located in the municipality of Anna (Valencia), is one of the most spectacular natural spots in the interior of the Valencian Community. It is a natural gorge carved by the Sellent River, with a natural pool of crystal-clear waters and a 25-meter waterfall. Access is via a steep staircase of 136 steps, hence its name.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike The Route of the 3 Waterfalls — Circular by the Albufera de Anna?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 5.7 km long and generally takes between 1.5 to 2 hours to complete, depending on your pace and how much time you spend enjoying the waterfalls and scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: Komoot classifies this as an easy hike, but the terrain is quite varied. While some sections are easily accessible, you'll encounter rocky, uneven, and potentially slippery areas, especially after rain. There are narrow paths, wooden footbridges, and a significant steep staircase with 136 steps leading to the Gorgo de la Escalera. Good hiking shoes are highly recommended.

faqs.question: Where can I park when visiting The Route of the 3 Waterfalls?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near the town of Anna. Specific parking information isn't provided in the data, but it's generally advisable to look for designated parking areas close to the main access points or the town center of Anna, from where the trail is usually accessible.

faqs.question: Is The Route of the 3 Waterfalls suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: While some parts are easy, the varied terrain, including slippery sections and the steep staircase at Gorgo de la Escalera, suggests it's best for children over 6 years old who are in good physical health. Supervision is essential, especially near water features and on uneven paths.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not explicitly state whether dogs are allowed or prohibited on this specific route. It's always best to check local regulations or signage at the trailhead regarding pets before you go.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is known for its stunning water features, which are particularly impressive after rainfall. However, wet conditions can make some sections slippery. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for hiking, while summer can be hot but provides a refreshing escape near the waterfalls. Consider visiting during cooler months to avoid crowds and intense heat.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to hike The Route of the 3 Waterfalls?
faqs.answer: Yes, visitors should be aware that there are access fees for this exclusive hiking route. It's recommended to check the official local sources for current pricing and any specific rules regarding entry before your visit.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is famous for its natural water features. You'll see the tranquil Albufera de Anna, the picturesque Gaspar Waterfall and Pool, the dramatic Vikings' Waterfall, and the impressive Salto Waterfall. The Gorgo de la Escalera, a natural gorge with a 25-meter waterfall, is also a significant highlight.

faqs.question: Can I swim in the natural pools and waterfalls along the trail?
faqs.answer: While the natural pools are very inviting, there are rules prohibiting swimming in certain areas. It's crucial to observe all local signage and regulations regarding swimming to ensure your safety and respect for the natural environment.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the La Canal trail.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied and sometimes slippery terrain, good hiking shoes are essential. A hiking stick can also be beneficial for stability on muddy or rocky sections. Don't forget water, snacks, sun protection, and a camera to capture the beautiful waterfalls.
